title: Pack a 4-byte function selector and one 32-byte argument into a single 16-byte mstore for compact calldata layout
description: When constructing calldata for an external call with one or two arguments, write the selector left-aligned in a 16-byte word with trailing zero padding (e.g. 0x205c2878000000000000000000000000 for withdrawTo). Then write arguments at subsequent offsets. The call(..., 0x10, 0x44, ...) reads from offset 0x10 for 0x44 bytes — exactly: 4-byte selector + 32-byte arg1 + 32-byte arg2. Avoids the standard right-align-and-shift dance for the selector.
pattern_before:
// Standard right-aligned selector
mstore(0x00, 0x205c2878) // selector at bytes [28..32]
mstore(0x20, to)
mstore(0x40, amount)
call(gas(), ep, 0, 0x1c, 0x44, ...) // start at 0x1c = 28
pattern_after:
// Left-aligned selector packed with leading zeros
mstore(0x14, to)     // address at [0x14..0x34]
mstore(0x34, amount) // amount at [0x34..0x54]
mstore(0x00, 0x205c2878000000000000000000000000) // selector at [0x10..0x14], zeros from [0..0x10]
call(gas(), ep, 0, 0x10, 0x44, ...)
mstore(0x34, 0) // restore overwritten free pointer slot
when_to_apply: External calls with 1-2 arguments where the calldata layout fits cleanly in scratch space, especially when the caller wants to keep all data in 0x00-0x60 region
when_not_to_apply: Calls with dynamic-length arguments or many parameters
